tavily.answer: Cameroon has adopted a maritime security law to combat piracy and other maritime offenses in the Gulf of Guinea, making it the second country in the region to enact such a legal framework. Meanwhile, questions have been raised over payments made by the UK Ministry of Defence to Jeffrey Donaldson for consultancy work on peace efforts in Cameroon, with critics questioning the necessity of additional payments while he was already receiving a parliamentary salary. The situation in Cameroon is further complicated by the broader regional crisis, as seen in Sudan where civil war and insecurity have pushed nearly 20 million people into acute hunger, with over 40% of the population struggling with food insecurity.

items.title: DRC Ebola hospital set on fire as protesters demand access to bodies of dead relatives - africanews.com
items.snippet: **Violence has erupted in eastern DR Congo's Ituri province after tensions linked to the handling of victims of the latest Ebola outbreak.**. Protesters in the town of Rwampara wanted to recover the bodies of relatives who died of Ebola in order to bury them.
